source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Three hexagons of increasing size are shown below. Suppose the dot pattern continues so that each successive hexagon contains one more band of dots. How many dots are in the next hexagon?

<image1>

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says "Three hexagons of increasing size are shown," so the core input is visual
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the problem is not solvable from text alone
solving requires reading the dot arrangement/bands in the image, not just extracting a textual fact
asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true and image quality metadata show the image is available and readable
multi_step: the task requires identifying the dot-count pattern across multiple shown hexagons and extrapolating to the next case
question_text refers to "each successive hexagon contains one more band of dots," which implies relational pattern reasoning rather than single-step lookup
the answer is numeric, but it is obtained after visual counting/structure recognition plus sequence continuation
metadata.multi_solution_policy.should_push_multi_solution_agent=true suggests stable multi-solution or multi-step potential in this dataset item
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text="37", giving a precise target for checking
the inferred process can be verified by counting dots in each shown hexagon and confirming the next hexagonal growth step
asset_registry_record.answer_manifest.answer_present=true and registry_passed=true reduce uncertainty about target validity
image_quality_summaries show high readability_score, supporting reliable downstream verification from the visual pattern

decision_reasons: assets are complete: image, question text, and numeric answer are all present with no registry issues
initial scoring is strong across image dependency, multi-step reasoning, and verifiability
the visual-pattern risk is manageable because the image is available and reportedly readable, so core semantics are recoverable and testable
the precise numeric answer supports reliable downstream verification
